tweets.text: We will not stop working until we get every hostage back home with their families and loved ones. https://t.co/rAVqZOC68Q
.@SecBlinken holds a press availability in Tel Aviv, Israel. Also streaming at: https://t.co/ETqs1kUPSz. https://t.co/uIGQx3tq2K

Henry Kissinger fled to the United States from Nazi Germany in 1938 at 15 years old. Serving two presidents as Secretary of State, he made countless history-bending decisions. A statesman in every sense of the word, he felt a duty and desire to serve America. https://t.co/asCvEKK1JI
We are focused on doing what we can to extend the humanitarian pause in Gaza. We remain committed to getting more hostages out and more humanitarian assistance in. https://t.co/stl1fuOhEi
This year, our U.S. embassies and consulates issued more than 10 million nonimmigrant visas for work, tourism, and education to benefit the U.S. economy. https://t.co/J8JGV0HBE2
NATO today is bigger, stronger, more united, more capable than ever. https://t.co/JGowurl30p

Over the last two days, @SecBlinken attended @NATO's Foreign Ministerial Meeting and met with with his counterparts from Norway, Turkey, Ukraine, and other Allies. As the Secretary said, NATO is “our greatest strategic asset: an unmatched network of alliances and partnerships.” https://t.co/7tL37HkjzO
.@‌SecBlinken holds a press availability in Brussels, Belgium. Also streaming at: https://t.co/IszetbFMod.  https://t.co/QHiHOt5Ses
Today, the United States airlifted more than 54,000 pounds of humanitarian supplies carrying food, medical supplies, and winter clothes to the people of Gaza.
The @NATO Alliance is more important than it’s ever been. @SecBlinken discusses the importance of NATO’s work and the Foreign Ministers Meeting. https://t.co/LrlHIDZfaO

.@POTUS: Faced with a threat to the peace and stability of the world, to democratic values we hold dear, to freedom itself, we did what we always do: The United States stepped up. NATO stepped up. https://t.co/Rca22P1GUN

Planning a trip abroad? Make sure you review the Travel Advisories for your intended destination. You can see the world at a glance on our interactive map. Learn more: #TravelSmartFromTheStart https://t.co/ZUIfgAm09L https://t.co/LlTuvvKic5

.@USAmbUN: We know that climate change has impacted food insecurity. It is something that we feel every day as we look at the patterns of heat across the globe, the patterns of flooding, intensified storms. We absolutely have to work together to address these issues today. https://t.co/zjQ20hzKIm
The urgency to address the climate crisis has never been greater. See how the @NatlParkService is tackling new problems as fires and natural disasters become more prevalent. https://t.co/U4g1Q1uzvK

Today is International Day for the Elimination of Violence against Women and the beginning of the #16DaysofActivism Against Gender-Based Violence. Gender-based violence is a human rights abuse and the United States is committed to ending all forms of gender-based violence. https://t.co/rwH8vq7Vta

The Holodomor was a man-made famine created by the Stalin regime to kill millions of Ukrainians and other Soviet citizens. On November 25, we remember those murdered in the Holodomor. As Russia continues to attack Ukraine and its food supplies, we remain #UnitedWithUkraine. https://t.co/sWF0KNepZH

The expertise of Indigenous youth can help shape a safer, more inclusive, and sustainable world for all. Meet six inspiring Indigenous youth leaders who are addressing global challenges at the local level, in this DipNote from @US_SRREJ. https://t.co/buRUPQJ6VD

On Thanksgiving, food is a key part of celebrating with loved ones. While sweet potatoes and pumpkin pies still abound, check out how a few Americans bring recipes from their ancestral homes to the Thanksgiving table. https://t.co/LDwZ0ctWnj

Today we celebrate Thanksgiving, a time to gather with friends and family and express gratitude. We are grateful for our State Department colleagues and our partners and allies, as we work together towards a better future. https://t.co/1Ek5TOjpdf

Sign up for the Smart Traveler Enrollment Program (STEP) to get up-to-date safety and security information in the event of an emergency abroad. #TravelSmartFromTheStart https://t.co/4m3cQyptBW https://t.co/LuCoGCeTm5

The United States welcomes the deal to release 50 hostages, including American citizens, held by Hamas since its October 7 attack on Israel. While this deal marks significant progress, we will not rest as long as Hamas continues to hold hostages in Gaza. - @SecBlinken https://t.co/5Y9J8rUFgp
Israelis and Palestinians equally deserve to live side by side in safety, dignity, and peace. - @POTUS https://t.co/ZPVnPy4iCh

The impacts of the climate crisis on Alaska’s salmon populations are real and visible, as our @SciDiplomacyUSA @vsfsatstate intern witnessed firsthand. Working together, we can support fishing communities and the survival of fish species we all depend on. https://t.co/KdGsvNUJU0
https://t.co/hAqHBdLv0e
.@USAmbChina Nicholas Burns recaps what @POTUS and People’s Republic of China President Xi Jinping discussed during their 4-hour meeting in San Francisco. https://t.co/9Otf5q1ZZ8

The world faces an inflection point, where the choices we make — including in the crises in Europe and the Middle East — will determine the direction of our future for generations to come. In the @washingtonpost, @POTUS shares his vision for a safer world. https://t.co/Hbb1fl68Sa

In “The Week at State,” @‌POTUS hosted @‌APEC Leaders’ Week in San Francisco and met with the People's Republic of China President Xi. @‌UnderSecStateJ traveled to Egypt to continue U.S. efforts to increase the flow of humanitarian assistance into Gaza. https://t.co/YESrsRKm87

On Women’s Entrepreneurship Day, the U.S. is proud to support women’s full and meaningful economic participation. Programs around the globe, including this one in Pakistan, are helping to create a world in which women and girls can make a real difference. https://t.co/IMuUiYShbe
We remain committed to a pathway for Palestinians and Israelis living side by side in states of their own–with equal measures of security, freedom, opportunity and dignity. https://t.co/RPCbrFuzUI
We’re standing up for our interests and values, not shrinking back. Not in the face of Russia’s aggression against Ukraine. - @SecBlinken https://t.co/9hlm2xXQrC

We stand for a secure, prosperous, and interconnected Black Sea Region, values more important than ever in the face of Russia’s aggression against Ukraine. @UnderSecPD shares how the Black Sea is critical for our @NATO Allies and the world, from the port of Varna in Bulgaria. https://t.co/N61RXbG6Xa

The U.S. is working with our Egyptian and international partners to ensure critical humanitarian assistance moves quickly into Gaza. This week, nearly 200 metric tons of @‌USAID food aid arrived near the Egypt-Gaza border. https://t.co/p6sCFY2W7L
U.S. Trade Representative @AmbassadorTai’s one word to sum up #USAPEC2023: wow! https://t.co/FF9FW5KN0B

We are restarting cooperation between the United States and the PRC on counternarcotics. We’re taking action to significantly reduce the flow of precursor chemicals and pill presses from China to the Western Hemisphere. It’s going to save lives. - @POTUS https://t.co/2Cdmfj3mCR

Through @APEC, the United States seeks to advance a free, fair, and open economic policy agenda that benefits U.S. workers, businesses, and families. Learn more: https://t.co/E25hzLb4Bc #USAPEC2023 https://t.co/rFLV2mGODb
.@APEC matters to the future of clean energy. We are focused on innovation and technology to address climate change and environmental sustainability. https://t.co/v0zkNQKHcp
Labor rights are key to our national security and foreign policy. - @SecBlinken https://t.co/sz3J79qjgg

We caught up with Senior Official for APEC, Ambassador Matt Murray, who explains how he got interested in the work @apec has been doing for over 30 years to promote open trade and investment. #USAPEC2023 https://t.co/Djb0sG98S1

The U.S. will continue to compete vigorously with the People's Republic of China. But we’ll manage that competition responsibly so it doesn’t veer into conflict or accidental conflict. And where it’s possible, where our interests coincide, we’re going to work together. - @POTUS https://t.co/AFxdeChPbO

.@USAID assistance of nearly 200 metric tons of food is arriving near the Egypt-Gaza border. That’s enough to feed 74,000 Palestinians. We know more needs to be done. We are working with our Egyptian and international partners to insure assistance moves more quickly into Gaza. https://t.co/Fl523P5qH7
U.S. food aid has arrived near the Egypt-Gaza border. @USAID and humanitarian aid partners are working to deliver it to the most vulnerable in Gaza as quickly as possible. https://t.co/htFIfNL5hT
Live now: @SecBlinken participates in the Global Labor Strategy Rollout in San Francisco, California. Also streaming at: https://t.co/b3WwhfCMwT https://t.co/grJkivoot7

Introducing the Bureau of Arms Control, Deterrence, and Stability, the new name of the Bureau of Arms Control, Verification and Compliance. It reflects the full scope of the bureau’s work and how it addresses new challenges posed by emerging security technologies and domains.

Ever wonder what a major economic forum for some of the world's top leaders looks like through the eyes of a U.S. Ambassador? Join @usembassytokyo Ambassador Rahm Emanuel to get his inside take on the APEC Economic Leaders' week. https://t.co/2SvHnhaMmk

The @USEmbassyCairo has helped more than 600 U.S. citizens and lawful permanent residents and their family members depart Gaza through the Rafah gate. We continue this important work, and appreciate the support of the governments of Egypt, Israel, and other partners. https://t.co/nJureGvNiv

Throughout the #USAPEC2023 host year, the United States has led engagements from Palm Springs to Detroit to Seattle. It all culminates this week in San Francisco as @POTUS and @SecBlinken welcome members for APEC Economic Leaders’ Week. https://t.co/hZlOdiMLQi
As the host of #USAPEC2023, the U.S. is focused on creating a resilient and sustainable future for all, including the positive impact international engagement has at home. https://t.co/8pn6x7s8fF

We simply could not do our work without our Foreign Service Nationals and Locally Employed Staff. They make up two-thirds of our workforce and help strengthen our relationship with countries around the world. Today we celebrate their invaluable contributions. https://t.co/IHNtp3PfaP

.@APEC member economies have experienced remarkable reciprocal benefits from greater trade and investment within the Asia Pacific region. At the same time, we continue to face many economic challenges.

We have to meet this moment head on. #USAPEC2023 https://t.co/Irc2LtGZUO

“Our engagement with @APEC underscores the U.S.’s enduring commitment to the vision that we all agreed in Malaysia in 2020: An open, dynamic, resilient and peaceful Asia Pacific community.” - @SecBlinken https://t.co/0AYsL8mJmW

Live now: @SecBlinken and @USTradeRep Katherine Tai co-host the APEC Ministerial Meeting Opening Plenary Session in San Francisco, California. Also streaming at: https://t.co/8A5QGF0E5F https://t.co/D2F2w2uVvM
Global public health is not some abstract policy debate, the decisions we make have real impacts on people around the world, and especially the most vulnerable among us. https://t.co/kvPGxUH51G

The annual #OpenDoorsReport on study abroad is out, and over 1 million students from around the world studied at U.S. colleges and universities, a 12% increase over the previous year. Check out https://t.co/4C9JXeJV0f to see how your school or country ranked. #IEW23 https://t.co/mBn9XqC6nQ

There is only one way to enhance global health security: together.

@SecBlinken outlines the critical role that our new Bureau of Global Health Security and Diplomacy plays in preparing the world for the next health threat. https://t.co/R4SRbfjySq

During @APEC Economic Leaders' Week, @POTUS will welcome Asia-Pacific leaders to San Francisco to cap off our successful #USAPEC2023 host year. Senior U.S. officials, including @SecBlinken, will be there to lead discussions on key priorities. https://t.co/BRSt9ynXpA

In “The Week at State,” @SecBlinken continues his travels with stops in Ramallah, Baghdad, Ankara, Tokyo for the G7 Ministers’ Meeting, Seoul, and New Delhi. Head over to https://t.co/58Uc9lIOt7 for the latest on all the Secretary’s travel. https://t.co/s3f47XSy1I

The United States will host @APEC's 2023 Economic Leaders’ Meeting in San Francisco this coming week. Here are three things you should know about how fair and inclusive trade across the Asia Pacific region impacts you. https://t.co/wbmE6YhrCo
Happy Diwali to communities around the world who are celebrating today. We wish you joy, peace, and success. https://t.co/AWzsFw62Gn

As @SecBlinken has said, the United States is working with partners in the Americas to advance safe, orderly, humane migration. Check out Diomaris and Alexis’ story on how the Safe Mobility initiative is supporting refugees & vulnerable migrants through safe and lawful pathways. https://t.co/SaAoH3OwPG
Today we honor the service, bravery, and sacrifice of our veterans. For generations, they have defended freedom, justice, and democracy. https://t.co/goOES0z69J

“Over the last 70 years, that relationship has grown from a key security alliance into a vital global partnership. On nearly every issue of consequence to our people, the United States and Korea are working closely together.” - @SecBlinken on the U.S.-ROK relationship. https://t.co/tNodsseN0W
All are welcome to explore our National Parks. Enjoy the natural beauty of the United States on Veterans Day when @NatlParkService offers free admission to everyone. https://t.co/a25s8V4yYi

.@POTUS: Together, India and the United States are working closely on everything from ending poverty and expanding access to healthcare to addressing climate change to tackling food and energy insecurity stoked by Russia’s unprovoked war on Ukraine. https://t.co/dejfuqkoWD

On nearly every issue of consequence to our people, the United States and the Republic of Korea are working closely together. Our two countries share an extraordinarily ambitious vision for the next generation of our partnership. https://t.co/zcTLCdlWvS

This year commemorates the 70th anniversary of the U.S.-ROK alliance, which advances peace and prosperity for both countries, the Indo-Pacific, and the world. Here’s a timeline since 1945. https://t.co/5jO1HVsrLb
At another moment of intense challenge for the free world, our security, and our values, @G7 unity is stronger and more important than ever. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/IkS31ngGxR

The Berlin Wall came to symbolize totalitarian tyranny and the Soviet Union’s division of Europe by force. Read more about how November 9, 1989 was a pivotal day for freedom and democracy as the Berlin Wall fell. https://t.co/UG64b2EhSB

To modernize diplomacy, we must lead by example to responsibly and ethically embrace AI. The Department's Enterprise AI Strategy is our commitment to leveraging and scaling trustworthy AI as a critical tool for the next era of American diplomacy. https://t.co/ICI8oZidhH
Ukraine can count on us. https://t.co/xvSJzoCDRn

We do not stand alone. We’ve built extraordinary coalitions with allies and partners who carry their share of the burden. And we leave this G7 meeting stronger and more united in meeting the challenges of today. 
-@SecBlinken https://t.co/jSwM6uBwP0
Even as we’re focused on the day of and getting through this crisis, we’re also focused on the day after and making sure that there’s a better path forward coming out of it. https://t.co/yAqpPkLjVX

“Palestinian civilians are not to blame for Hamas’s atrocities or for the grave humanitarian crisis in Gaza. They are its victims. As with civilians in any conflict, the lives of Palestinian civilians must be protected.” - @SecBlinken https://t.co/GP56uf8Jbx

We all recognize that we cannot go back to the status quo. We all understand that we not only have an interest, but a responsibility, to do everything we can to chart a better path forward together. - @SecBlinken https://t.co/O6cIHcqciv
The United States remains deeply committed to working with partners in the region to end this conflict in a way that ensures lasting peace and security in the Middle East. https://t.co/bwi0jMtlsZ

In Ankara, the Secretary met with Turkish Foreign Minister Fidan on preventing the spread of the Israel-Hamas conflict and the importance of our longstanding partnership in addressing regional and global challenges. https://t.co/AJufB1SxkP

In Baghdad, the Secretary met with Iraqi Prime Minister Al-Sudani to discuss the need to prevent the conflict between Israel and Hamas from spreading, and urged the Prime Minister to hold accountable those responsible for attacks targeting U.S. personnel in Iraq and Syria. https://t.co/hyMlIr4ReK

In Ramallah, the Secretary met with Palestinian Authority President Abbas, where he made clear that Palestinians must not be displaced and extremist violence in the West Bank must end. https://t.co/cy894ISg8G

In Amman, he met with regional partners on what we can do to set the conditions for a durable, sustainable, lasting peace for Israelis and Palestinians. The Secretary also spoke with @UNRWA staff to hear firsthand about their lifesaving work in Gaza. https://t.co/sZYF7Cp44c

In Tel Aviv, he met with Israeli officials to reiterate that Israel has the right to defend itself, but the way it does so matters. The Secretary discussed prioritizing the safety of civilians and getting humanitarian assistance into Gaza. https://t.co/bmhlbNK86h

.@SecBlinken wrapped his four-day trip focused on the Israel-Hamas conflict, where he met with regional leaders on our work to prevent the conflict from spreading, protect Palestinian civilians, and increase the flow of humanitarian assistance. 🧵 https://t.co/FARhm9GpGv

In “The Week at State,” @‌SecBlinken heads back to the Middle East region to engage in intensive diplomacy. We also celebrate this year’s winners for the Secretary’s Award for Corporate Excellence and @‌StateGWI unveils the 2023 #WPS Strategy. https://t.co/NAQvCUnmwS

The United States continues to believe that the sole viable path is a two-state solution, with Israelis and Palestinians each exercising their legitimate right to live in a state of their own, with equal measures of security and freedom, of opportunity and dignity. https://t.co/S9u4GX7HTj
.@SecBlinken discusses tangible steps being taken to increase the sustained delivery of food, water, medicine, fuel, and other essentials into Gaza. https://t.co/WPHLW3Is2X

“We are focused on setting the conditions for durable and sustainable peace and security. The United States continues to believe the best viable path – the only viable path – is through a two-state solution.” 
-@SecBlinken https://t.co/SibSg1I2yD

Live now: @SecBlinken holds a joint press availability with Egyptian Foreign Minister Sameh Shoukry and Jordanian Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ayman Safadi in Amman, Jordan.
Also streaming at: https://t.co/LSmsJujhef. https://t.co/g8Q9KrHJto

.@SecBlinken emphasizes the need to increase humanitarian assistance into Gaza and tangible steps for the sustained delivery of food, water, medicine, fuel, and other essential needs. https://t.co/UQo0dBtgeM

.@SecBlinken returned to Tel Aviv today to engage in intense diplomacy. 
"It is now, in this darkest moment, that we must fight hardest to preserve a path of stability, security, opportunity, integration, prosperity – and peace. Not tomorrow. Not after this war. Today." https://t.co/DMNv9bzSZR

“Since we reached agreement with Israel, Egypt, and the @UN two weeks ago on mechanisms to enable humanitarian aid to begin reaching civilians in need, we have scaled up deliveries." - @SecBlinken on getting essentials into Gaza https://t.co/3VBqvbpJjZ
"We need to continue to prevent escalation of this conflict." - @SecBlinken https://t.co/RuE8Vmpe4V
Civilians in Gaza should not suffer the consequences for Hamas's inhumanity and its brutality. https://t.co/1GwuOFtubf
.@SecBlinken shares our vision that we can forge a better tomorrow for both Israelis and Palestinians. https://t.co/IPIOqs6C48
Live now: @SecBlinken holds a press availability in Tel Aviv, Israel. Also streaming at: https://t.co/ngOd83LKJR https://t.co/vTr8vSwMOe

“We’re determined to prevent escalation, to prevent the spread of this conflict, and we’re taking necessary steps to try to make sure that that happens.”
- @SecBlinken https://t.co/VcSUkOyaPV
Israelis and Palestinians equally deserve to live side by side in safety, dignity, and peace. - @POTUS https://t.co/TZw0nVacCy

Helping prevent a worsening humanitarian catastrophe aligns with our nation’s most deeply held principles, including our belief that every civilian life is equally valuable, equally worthy of protection. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/xywhqJU7DR

The U.S. continues to advocate for open and free press around the world and accountability for those who attack press freedom. On International Day to End Impunity for Crimes against Journalists, we honor these brave journalists who continue to hold governments to account. https://t.co/cICQwKjpsh

Today an initial group of foreign nationals, including U.S. citizens, departed Gaza through the Rafah Crossing. Additionally, the United States is accelerating the delivery of humanitarian aid into Gaza — with a total of 217 trucks having now entered. https://t.co/n86kbhfEr2

Providing immediate aid and protection for Palestinian civilians in the conflict is also a necessary foundation for finding partners in Gaza who have a different vision for the future than Hamas — and who are willing to help make it real. https://t.co/oAGgFuqyQT https://t.co/8COi6oMTGz

During National Native American Heritage Month, we honor the profound impact Native Americans continue to have in shaping our country and our diplomacy efforts around the world. The culture and heritage of Indigenous peoples make our country stronger. https://t.co/UcUgXchH36

Today, @SecBlinken testified before Congress where he outlined how the United States continues leading in the world, working with our allies and partners to build coalitions and providing steadfast support to Ukraine and Israel. https://t.co/rUv7RWRsHC

Today, @SecBlinken released the U.S. Strategy and National Action Plan on Women, Peace and Security. Implementation of the #WomenPeaceSecurity agenda is both a moral and strategic imperative of U.S. foreign policy and national security.

.@POTUS’s Executive Order on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Artificial Intelligence further demonstrates our commitment to harness the opportunities of AI while mitigating its risks. https://t.co/m5IQP8Kezg
